skill.name: prisma-v7
skill.description: Guides users through Prisma ORM v7 setup, schema design, migrations, client generation, driver adapters, and migration from Prisma v6.
skill.summary: Practical Prisma ORM v7 guidance for TypeScript database projects.
skill.author: DjordjeVuckovic

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, credential, filesystem, network, and weak-cryptography patterns. Review found these are documentation examples for Prisma setup, migrations, database URLs, and official Prisma links, with no executable script, prompt injection, obfuscation, or exfiltration intent. Publishable with a low-risk note because users may run database-changing CLI commands from the examples.

low_findings.title: Documented Prisma CLI Commands
low_findings.description: The static command-execution findings are true code examples but false positives for skill execution. The file lists Prisma and npm commands for setup, generation, migration, reset, validation, studio, and seeding; these are relevant to the skill but can change a user database if run without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in fenced documentation blocks and match Prisma workflows. They are not executed by the skill, but several commands can modify or reset databases if a user runs them.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable and Database URL Examples
low_findings.description: The environment and database connection findings are legitimate Prisma examples using placeholders and process.env access. No hardcoded real secret was found, and the skill explicitly advises not committing .env files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines show standard Prisma configuration and placeholder connection strings. The surrounding guidance treats secrets as environment variables and does not transmit them.

low_findings.title: Official Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to Prisma documentation pages. They do not collect data, call webhooks, or instruct posting secrets to a third-party endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are clear-text references to official prisma.io documentation. No code path sends local data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Relative Import Path Misread as Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal finding is a TypeScript import from a generated Prisma client in a seed example. It is a normal relative module import, not user-controlled filesystem traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only traversal-like sequence is ../ in an import path inside a code sample. There is no file read, file write, or user input path construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography and reconnaissance labels arise from ordinary Prisma documentation terms, URLs, ordering examples, IDs, and schema fields. No cryptographic API, host probing, port scan, or system inventory behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation text and schema examples at the cited areas, not calls to crypto libraries or reconnaissance tools. The suspicious combination heuristic is explained by co-located documentation examples.

dangerous_patterns.title: Database-Changing Commands in Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands such as migrate reset, db push, db seed, and migrate deploy. These are expected Prisma operations, but users should verify environment targets before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and can change database state. Risk is bounded because they are examples, not automatically executed code.

content.value_statement: Prisma v7 changes client generation, configuration, adapters, and migrations. This skill gives direct guidance for setup, migration, and safe database workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains Prisma v7 ESM requirements and TypeScript configuration.
Shows driver adapter setup for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and serverless providers.
Guides prisma.config.ts usage and Prisma Client generation paths.
Provides migration workflows for development, production, and v6 upgrades.
Gives examples for relations, CRUD queries, transactions, seeding, and performance tuning.
Lists troubleshooting steps for module resolution, connection errors, and failed migrations.
limitations: Does not inspect a live database or verify schema state automatically.
Does not replace Prisma official documentation for provider-specific edge cases.
Examples use placeholder credentials and must be adapted before production use.
MongoDB guidance is limited because the skill focuses on Prisma v7 relational workflows.

use_cases.title: Plan a Prisma v7 Upgrade
use_cases.description: Map Prisma v6 configuration, adapters, client output, and migration commands to the v7 workflow.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New TypeScript API
use_cases.description: Create a Prisma schema, configure ESM, choose a database adapter, and generate a typed client.

use_cases.title: Review Database Workflow Issues
use_cases.description: Check migration, seeding, connection pooling, and query patterns before a release.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Prisma v7 Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Prisma v7 for a TypeScript project using PostgreSQL. Include required files, adapter choice, and the first migration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Schema to v7
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Prisma v6 schema and explain the changes needed for Prisma v7, including generator output, datasource configuration, and client imports.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Client and Adapter Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: I get a Prisma v7 client import or connection error. Help me diagnose ESM setup, generated client path, dotenv loading, and adapter configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production Prisma v7 migration plan for my service. Include staging checks, deploy commands, rollback considerations, and database safety notes.

output_examples.input: User asks how to upgrade from Prisma v6 to v7.
output_examples.output: A migration checklist covering package updates, ESM setup, driver adapter installation, prisma.config.ts changes, client output updates, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: User asks why Prisma Client cannot connect after moving to v7.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting path that checks DATABASE_URL loading, adapter configuration, generated client imports, database availability, and pool settings.

output_examples.input: User asks for a safe production migration workflow.
output_examples.output: A release sequence that separates local migration creation, staging validation, production deploy commands, status checks, and post-deploy verification.

best_practices: Verify the target database environment before running migration or reset commands.
Keep database credentials in environment variables and never commit .env files.
Run generate, validate, and staging migrations before production deployment.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder database URLs into production without replacing credentials and hosts.
Do not run migrate reset against shared, staging, or production databases.
Do not mix Prisma Accelerate URLs with standard driver adapters.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps with Prisma ORM v7 setup, migration, schema design, client generation, adapters, queries, and troubleshooting.

faq.question: Can it run Prisma commands for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides command guidance, but users must review and run commands in their own environment.

faq.question: Does Prisma v7 require ESM?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explains the package and TypeScript settings needed for an ESM project.

faq.question: Does it support Prisma v6 migrations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for moving v6 projects to v7 configuration and client generation patterns.

faq.question: Does it handle secrets safely?
faq.answer: It recommends environment variables and warns against committing .env files. It does not need real credentials.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
